skills.name: Technical Discovery & Needs Analysis
skills.description: The ability to conduct structured technical discovery sessions with customers to uncover their current environment, pain points, integration landscape, and business requirements. Involves asking the right questions, mapping technical stacks, and identifying gaps between current state and desired outcomes.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can follow a discovery framework to gather basic technical requirements from customers. Documents findings clearly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads discovery sessions independently, identifying unstated requirements and technical risks. Translates findings into solution recommendations.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs discovery methodologies for the team. Uncovers strategic business drivers behind technical requirements. Identifies complex multi-system dependencies and architectures.

skills.name: Solution Design & Architecture
skills.description: Designing tailored technical solutions that map product capabilities to customer business requirements. Involves creating architecture diagrams, integration plans, data flow designs, and implementation roadmaps that address specific customer environments and constraints.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design basic integration architectures using standard product configurations. Follows established solution patterns.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs end-to-end solutions for complex customer environments involving multiple systems, APIs, and data flows. Creates architecture documentation and implementation plans.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise-grade solutions across complex multi-system landscapes. Designs reference architectures that become reusable assets. Evaluates trade-offs across performance, security, cost, and scalability.

skills.name: Product Demonstration & Technical Storytelling
skills.description: The art of delivering compelling product demonstrations that connect technical capabilities to customer business outcomes. Involves tailoring demos to specific audiences (technical vs. executive), whiteboarding solutions, and creating narrative arcs that build conviction and drive technical wins.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Delivers standard product demos following a script. Can answer basic technical questions during demos.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Tailors demos to specific customer use cases and pain points. Comfortable whiteboarding and adjusting on the fly based on audience reactions. Handles objections effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates demo environments and narratives that build champions. Delivers impromptu whiteboard sessions to C-level audiences. Designs demo strategies that win competitive evaluations.

skills.name: Proof of Concept (POC) Design & Execution
skills.description: Planning, executing, and managing proof-of-concept engagements that validate solution fit and demonstrate business value. Includes defining success criteria, managing timelines, coordinating resources, handling technical blockers, and driving POCs to a clear win/no-win decision.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Supports POC execution by handling specific technical tasks and documenting results. Follows POC plans created by senior team members.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and runs POCs end-to-end, defining success criteria with customers, managing timelines, and presenting results. Converts POC wins into deal momentum.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs POC frameworks and methodologies for the SE organization. Manages complex multi-phase evaluations against competitors. Achieves consistently high POC-to-close conversion rates.

skills.name: Technical Onboarding & Implementation
skills.description: Owning the technical aspects of customer onboarding from contract signing through go-live. Involves configuring products, setting up integrations, managing data migrations, training customer teams, and ensuring customers achieve first value quickly. Focused on reducing time-to-value.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Executes onboarding tasks within established playbooks. Configures standard integrations and guides customers through setup steps.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads end-to-end onboarding for complex customers, managing multiple stakeholders and technical workstreams. Designs automated onboarding workflows and self-service resources.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds and optimizes onboarding methodologies and playbooks for the organization. Defines success metrics and continuously improves time-to-value. Handles the most complex, multi-system implementations.

skills.name: Integration & Middleware Platforms
skills.description: Hands-on experience with integration platforms, middleware, and workflow automation tools (Zapier, Make, Workato, n8n, MuleSoft) to connect systems, move data between platforms, and build automated workflows that solve customer requirements beyond native product integrations.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can set up basic integrations using standard connectors on one or two middleware platforms. Understands webhook concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds complex multi-step automated workflows across multiple systems. Troubleshoots integration failures and optimizes data flows. Works with webhooks, APIs, and custom connectors.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise integration strategies involving multiple middleware platforms. Designs custom connectors and complex event-driven workflows. Advises customers on integration architecture best practices.

skills.name: Competitive Positioning & Technical Differentiation
skills.description: Understanding the competitive landscape and articulating technical differentiation against specific competitors in sales situations. Involves maintaining knowledge of competitor products, positioning strengths against weaknesses, and handling competitive objections during evaluations.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic competitive positioning for the company's primary competitors. Can reference standard battlecards during sales conversations.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Articulates detailed technical differentiation against multiple competitors. Designs POC criteria that highlight product strengths. Handles competitive objections confidently in customer meetings.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Maintains deep expertise across the competitive landscape. Creates competitive intelligence materials for the SE team. Wins head-to-head technical evaluations against strong competitors consistently.

skills.name: Technical Content & Enablement Creation
skills.description: Creating technical content that enables customers, partners, and internal teams. Includes reference architectures, best practices guides, demo applications, knowledge bases, FAQs, video tutorials, and training materials that scale the SE function's impact.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic documentation, FAQs, and knowledge base articles. Maintains existing technical content.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops reference architectures, how-to guides, and demo applications that are used by the broader SE team. Creates partner enablement materials.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs the content strategy for the SE organization. Creates reusable assets, templates, and automations that scale delivery. Builds community-facing content that drives brand awareness.

skills.name: Customer Technical Relationship Management
skills.description: Building and maintaining deep technical relationships with customer stakeholders across multiple levels (engineers, architects, IT leaders, executives). Involves becoming a trusted technical advisor, managing expectations, navigating organizational politics, and ensuring long-term customer technical success.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Builds rapport with technical contacts and maintains regular communication. Responds effectively to customer technical inquiries.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops trusted advisor relationships with multiple stakeholders including technical leads and directors. Proactively identifies customer needs and expansion opportunities. Manages technical escalations smoothly.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds executive-level technical relationships with CIOs, CTOs, and VPs of Engineering. Creates technical champions who advocate internally. Navigates complex multi-stakeholder organizations and political dynamics.

skills.name: Solutions Engineering Team Leadership
skills.description: Leading and scaling a Solutions Engineering team, including hiring, mentoring, performance management, and process development. Involves building SE methodologies, defining KPIs, managing capacity across deals, and creating a culture of technical excellence and customer obsession.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Mentors junior SEs informally and contributes to hiring interviews. Helps define team processes.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a team of 3-8 SEs, running regular 1:1s, setting goals, and tracking KPIs. Builds onboarding processes for new SE hires. Distributes deal support across the team effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Scales SE organizations across regions and domains. Designs SE career ladders, compensation models, and operating rhythms. Builds and enables partner delivery programs. Aligns SE strategy with company revenue targets.

skills.name: Delivery Methodology & Process Design
skills.description: Designing and operationalizing repeatable delivery processes for customer implementations. Includes creating project frameworks, QA standards, launch readiness checklists, escalation paths, success metrics, and playbooks that enable consistent, high-quality delivery at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Follows established delivery processes and identifies areas for improvement. Documents learnings from implementations.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs delivery playbooks and processes for specific product lines or customer segments. Defines success metrics and tracks delivery quality.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds the delivery methodology for the organization from scratch. Creates frameworks that scale from high-touch to low-touch delivery. Implements tooling and automation to improve delivery efficiency and margins.

skills.name: Technical Sales & Business Value Articulation
skills.description: The ability to bridge technical capabilities and business outcomes in sales conversations. Involves building ROI models, translating features into business value, supporting pricing and scoping decisions, and partnering effectively with Account Executives to drive revenue.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands the connection between product features and customer business outcomes. Can support AEs with technical context during sales conversations.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds ROI-driven value propositions using customer data and performance metrics. Partners proactively with AEs on deal strategy. Supports commercial scoping and effort estimation.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Shapes deal strategy at the executive level. Designs commercial models for SE-delivered services. Manages delivery margins and resource allocation across a portfolio of accounts.

skills.name: RFP/RFI/RFQ Response Management
skills.description: Responding to formal customer procurement documents including Requests for Proposal (RFPs), Requests for Information (RFIs), and Requests for Quotation (RFQs). Involves interpreting requirements, coordinating input from multiple internal teams, and crafting compelling technical responses.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Contributes technical sections to RFP responses. Follows established templates and response libraries.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads the technical response for standard RFPs, coordinating across teams and tailoring responses to customer requirements.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages complex, multi-product RFP responses for strategic accounts. Builds and maintains response libraries and templates that improve win rates.
